Title: DeltaSpark receives CHF 150'000 from Venture Kick
Article_Text: DeltaSpark receives CHF 150'000 from Venture Kick 31.03.2026 The EPFL spin-off DeltaSpark has obtained CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick to advance a technology that converts CO₂ into stable minerals while producing cheap and clean hydrogen on-site. The cleantech startup aims to help industries reduce emissions while generating valuable byproducts. Record-high CO₂ emissions are driving global warming and increasing pressure on industries to reduce their carbon footprint. Sectors such as cement, waste-to-energy, and heavy manufacturing face particular challenges, as their emissions are difficult to eliminate. Current carbon capture solutions often store CO₂ without creating additional value, limiting their economic attractiveness. EPFL spin-off DeltaSpark created a compact system that converts CO₂ into solid, stable minerals. Using an electrocatalytic process that is up to 100 times faster than other existing methods, the technology simultaneously produces clean hydrogen and oxygen. By turning emissions into useful resources, this approach offers industries a way to reduce carbon output while generating useful chemicals and materials. DeltaSpark’s CO₂-offset subscription aims to be priced below carbon taxes, allowing large emitters, such as cement plants and incinerators, to cut CO₂ liabilities while gaining strategic access to high-value chemicals. The Venture Kick philanthropic initiative provides seed funding to Swiss startups and offers a well-structured entrepreneurial path toward building a business. The CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick will enable DeltaSpark to accelerate the delivery of its paid pilots and support its seed fundraising round in the next months. “Venture Kick is more than just funding. It also provided us with essential coaching that helped us refine our business plan for our target customer segments,” highlights CEO Luc Bondaz. “Through this support, we were able to capture our customers’ needs, sharpen our value proposition, and develop a clear go-to-market strategy.” DeltaSpark was founded by CEO Dr. Luc Bondaz and CTO Dr. Suhas Nuggehalli Sampathkumar in late 2025. Supported by advisors with expertise in scale-up and process engineering, the team combines technical and industrial knowledge to bring carbon conversion solutions to market. Title: NanoZymeX receives CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick
Article_Text: NanoZymeX receives CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick 25.03.2026 The biotech startup NanoZymeX has obtained CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick to advance a new platform for enzyme replacement therapy using lipid nanoparticles. The technology aims to improve treatment options for rare genetic conditions by increasing therapeutic activity in affected tissues. Enzyme replacement therapies are used to treat rare genetic disorders, but their effectiveness is often limited by poor delivery to the target tissue. In addition, immune responses to repeated treatments can limit long-term effectiveness. NanoZymeX is developing a lipid nanoparticle-based platform designed to transport therapeutic enzymes directly into target cells and lysosomes. By improving intracellular delivery and reducing immune reactions, the approach increases functional activity where it is most needed. Early preclinical studies show strong delivery efficiency and improved enzyme activity in relevant tissues. NanoZymeX’s technology targets lysosomal storage disorders, a group of rare diseases representing a multibillion-dollar therapeutic market, such as Pompe disease, an inherited lysosomal disorder. The University of Basel spin-off is led by Boris Sevarika, Margarita C. Dinamarca, and Eva Hemmrich, who each bring complementary expertise in formulation development, neurobiology, and regulatory science. “The support from Venture Kick comes at a critical moment when scientific discoveries need to transition into real companies,” highlighted Boris Sevarika, co-founder of NanoZymeX. “This type of early funding provides the flexibility needed for company building, business development, and preparing the next stages of financing. It fills a crucial gap between academic research and venture-backed biotech development.” The Venture Kick philanthropic initiative provides seed funding to Swiss startups and offers a well-structured entrepreneurial path toward building a business. The CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick will help NanoZymeX further develop its lipid nanoparticle technology, conduct additional preclinical studies, and prepare scalable manufacturing. This will support future fundraising and the transition toward clinical trials. Title: Migros-Testlauf für YourStarter
Article_Text: Migros-Testlauf für YourStarter 30.03.2026 YourStarter hat seine Frühstücksdrinks bereits bei über 50'000 Privatkundinnen und -kunden sowie mehreren hundert Unternehmen etabliert. Jetzt setzen die St. Galler ihren Fokus auf den Detailhandel: Nach der Aufnahme ins Sortiment in mehreren Coop-Filialen in der Ostschweiz startet das nächste grosse Detailhandelsunternehmen eine Testphase. Die Produkte sind nun in 50 Migros-Filialen erhältlich. Vor etwas mehr als zwei Jahren gründeten die Brüder Fabian und Nando Lüchinger das Startup YourStarter. Die Idee entstand aus ihrem persönlichen Interesse an gesunder Ernährung, gepaart mit der Erfahrung, dass im hektischen Alltag oft die Zeit für ein ausgewogenes Frühstück fehlt. «Gerade am Morgen muss es meistens schnell gehen. Gleichzeitig können gesunde Produkte geschmacklich häufig nicht mit ungesünderen Alternativen mithalten. Genau dafür wollten wir eine Lösung schaffen – ohne beim Geschmack oder bei der Qualität Kompromisse einzugehen», erklärt Fabian Lüchinger. YourStarter bietet Frühstücksmischungen im Becher an: Der Inhalt wird mit Wasser oder Milch vermischt, wodurch ein fertiger Frühstücksdrink entsteht. Die Starter bestehen aus natürlichen Bio-Zutaten wie Früchte, Nüsse und Haferflocken. Sie kommen ohne Zusatzstoffe, Süssungsmittel oder E-Nummern aus und liefern Proteine, Ballaststoffe und Vitamine. Das mittlerweile 20-köpfige Team produziert die Produkte in der eigenen Produktionsstätte im St. Galler Rheintal in Oberriet. Bislang wurde die Entwicklung des Startups vollständig aus eigenen Mitteln finanziert. Verkauft wird über drei Kanäle: Der eigene Onlineshop, über den bereits mehr als 50’000 Kundinnen und Kunden direkt bestellt haben, ist laut Lüchinger aktuell der grösste Vertriebskanal. Gleichzeitig ist der Firmenbereich in den letzten Monaten stark gewachsen. Über 300 Unternehmen stellen ihren Mitarbeitenden die Starter für den Arbeitsalltag zur Verfügung. Auch im Detailhandel sind die Drinks angekommen. In mehreren Coop-Filialen in der Ostschweiz sind die Produkte bereits erhältlich, weitere Standorte folgen Schritt für Schritt. Um die Präsenz im Detailhandel weiter auszubauen, hat YourStarter nun eine sechsmonatige Testphase mit der Migros gestartet. Die Starter sind in 50 Filialen in der Ostschweiz erhältlich, mit dem Ziel, anschliessend dauerhaft ins Sortiment aufgenommen zu werden und das Angebot gegebenenfalls auszuweiten. «Wir sind da sehr zuversichtlich. Auch die ersten Zahlen, die wir bisher bereits nach so kurzer Zeit erhalten haben, sehen sehr gut aus», so Lüchinger. Für das laufende Jahr plant YourStarter ausserdem, den gesamten DACH-Raum zu beliefern und die Marke über die Schweiz hinaus zu etablieren. Title: Proptech nestermind erfolgreich mit KI-Software
Article_Text: Proptech nestermind erfolgreich mit KI-Software 23.03.2026 Vor einem Jahr gestartet, hat nestermind bereits einen Umsatz im mittleren sechsstelligen Bereich erreicht. Die modulare Lösung des Startups automatisiert zentrale Prozesse im Immobilienverkauf auch mit Hilfe von KI-Agenten. Das Zürcher Startup nestermind entwickelt eine KI-gestützte PropTech-Software, die Prozesse im Immobilienverkauf automatisiert und strukturiert abbildet. Die Software unterstützt Maklerinnen und Makler entlang zentraler Schritte des Verkaufsprozesses und automatisiert Aufgaben wie Lead-Generierung, Immobilienvermarktung und CRM. Mit dem Smart Listing Manager werden Immobilienanzeigen auf verschiedenen Plattformen optimiert, personalisierte Follow-ups automatisch erstellt und die zweiseitige Lead-Generierung verbindet Maklerinnen und Makler mit passenden Käuferinnen, Käufern und potenziellen Verkäuferinnen und Verkäufern. Auch organisatorische Aufgaben werden automatisiert: Für Besichtigungen schlägt das System passende Zeitfenster vor, die mit dem Kalender der Maklerinnen und Makler abgestimmt werden. Für Premium- und Booster-Kunden stehen zudem neue Funktionen zur Verfügung. Dazu gehören Smart Messaging für automatisierte, kontextbasierte Antworten, ein Voice AI Assistant, der unter anderem das Erstellen von Verkaufsunterlagen und das Versenden von Objektinformationen übernimmt, sowie eine Conversation Intelligence, die alle Interaktionen mit Interessentinnen und Interessenten strukturiert zusammenfasst und detaillierte Profile für den Verkaufsprozess erstellt. Seit dem Go-Live im zweiten Quartal 2025 verzeichnet nestermind in der Schweiz ein kontinuierliches Wachstum. Über 8’000 Objekte sind mittlerweile auf nestermind erfasst und mehr als 250 Immobilienprofis nutzen die Lösung regelmässig. «In einzelnen Regionen der Schweiz wurden bereits über 10% aller auf grossen Immobilienportalen veröffentlichten Objekte über nestermind inseriert», erklärt Co-Founder und CEO Lucas Pelloni. Gleichzeitig hat nestermind bereits mehrere hunderttausend Franken Umsatz erzielt. Die Expansion ins Ausland geht Pelloni gezielt an und arbeitet mit ersten Pilotkunden in Baden-Württemberg und auf den Balearen zusammen. «In den letzten Monaten haben wir vermehrt Interesse aus dem Ausland erhalten, mussten jedoch viele dieser Anfragen ablehnen, um uns konsequent auf den Schweizer Markt zu fokussieren und dort eine starke Basis aufzubauen. Die aktuellen Partnerschaften sind nun ein erster, gezielter Schritt, um dieses internationale Interesse selektiv aufzugreifen und gemeinsam mit ausgewählten Unternehmen erste Erfahrungen in neuen Märkten zu sammeln», so Lucas Pelloni. Die Pilotkunden sollen nestermind unterstützen, die Internationalisierungsstrategie fundiert weiterzuentwickeln. Kurzfristig bleibt der Fokus jedoch klar auf der Schweiz, bevor die Expansion ins Ausland schrittweise ausgeweitet wird. Title: OOMNIUM sichert sich 870'000 Franken über eigene Plattform
Article_Text: OOMNIUM sichert sich 870'000 Franken über eigene Plattform 01.04.2026 Die Finanzierungsplattform OOMNIUM will verstärkt die Möglichkeiten von KI nutzen und plant zudem, einen digitalen Business-Angel-Club aufzubauen. Um sich die dafür notwendigen frischen Mittel zu sichern, setzte OOMNIUM auf die eigene Plattform. Mit Erfolg: das ursprüngliche Ziel von 600000 Franken wurde deutlich übertroffen. Vor drei Jahren ist OOMNIUM an den Start gegangen. Seit einem Jahr gibt es nebst den öffentlichen Kampagnen auch private auf der Plattform, bei denen gezielt ausgewählte Investorinnen und Investoren angesprochen werden. Dieses Modell hat OOMNIUM nun erfolgreich selbst genutzt: Die Eigenkampagne wurde Mitte März nach 17 Tagen abgeschlossen und lag deutlich über dem ursprünglichen Ziel von 600’000 Franken. «Wir versprechen den Unternehmen, die mit uns Kapital aufnehmen, schnellere und erfolgreichere Finanzierungsrunden. Mit unserer eigenen Runde haben wir bewiesen, dass unsere Methodik funktioniert», so CEO und Co-Founder Leandro Davies. An der Runde beteiligten sich 32 Personen aus den persönlichen Netzwerken des Teams sowie ausgewählte Investorinnen und Investoren der OOMNIUM-Basis. Ausgegeben wurden sowohl traditionell ausgestellte Namensaktien als auch tokenisierte Partizipationsscheine – abhängig von Phase und Höhe des Investments. Die Runde war in drei Phasen aufgeteilt, die jeweils zwischen fünf und sieben Tagen dauerten. Das Mindestticket lag je nach Phase zwischen 10'000 und 45'000 Franken, wobei die effektiven Beträge in vielen Fällen deutlich darüber lagen. Die Mehrheit der Unternehmensanteile und Stimmrechte ist weiterhin bei den Gründungsaktionärinnen und -aktionären. Mit den frischen Mitteln sollen die Prozesse auf der Plattform weiter automatisiert werden, unter anderem durch den Einsatz von künstlicher Intelligenz. Gleichzeitig investiert OOMNIUM in den Aufbau und die Lancierung des digitalen Business-Angel-Clubs «OOMNIUM Circle». «Wir merken, dass viele Next-Gen-Business Angels andere Ansprüche an die Art und Weise haben, wie und in welchem Rahmen sie ihr Geld anlegen. Dem wollen wir mit dem Aufbau des «OOMNIUM Circle» Rechnung tragen.», erklärt Iva Bozovic, CMO bei OOMNIUM. Zudem rechnet OOMNIUM damit, die Profitabilitätsgrenze noch im Laufe des Jahres zu erreichen. Kurzfristig legt das Team den Fokus auf die Umsetzung: Mehr als ein Dutzend Startups sollen noch im ersten Halbjahr über die Plattform Kapital aufnehmen. (press release / LF) Bild: (v.l.n.r) Arijana Walcott (VR), Cécile Simoness (Campaign Lead), Franz D'Huc (Co-Founder, VRP), Rea Eggli (Co-Founder, VR), Leandro Davies (Co-Founder, CEO), Ruben Feurer (COO) und Iva Bozovic (CMO) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about OOMNIUM AG Related News OOMNIUM AG 09.02.2026 16:00 OOMNIUM verdoppelt Investmentvolumen 31.05.2023 16:15 Crowdinvestment trägt im Mai üppige Blüten Related tags

Title: 1.8M Seed Round to fuel SolidWatts Industrial Process Heating Vision
Article_Text: 1.8M Seed Round to fuel SolidWatts Industrial Process Heating Vision 26.03.2026 SolidWatts announces the closure of CHF 1.8 million Seed funding to accelerate the development and market entry of its innovative industrial process heating technology. This seed funding will be used to scale SolidWatts’ platform to higher power levels and to conduct pilots with industrial partners and clients actively seeking to reduce their energy consumption and expenditure by electrifying heating processes. Pully-based SolidWatts is on a mission to revolutionize industrial heating. Its proprietary, cutting-edge solid-state Radio Frequency (RF) platform delivers high power dielectric heating at efficiencies and scale that make it a direct replacement for traditional fossil fuel-based processes - for the first time. With a focus on sustainability and energy efficiency, and with support from the Swiss Climate Foundation, the company is dedicated to helping power-hungry industries transition to clean, electrified, solutions. Applicable to traditional industries and emerging ones, SolidWatts’ technology enables energy-intensive industries, such as chemical and pharmaceutical, food processing, carbon capture and advanced recycling. SolidWatts’ platform enables these sectors to electrify their processes, cutting both energy consumption and CO₂ emissions, improving overall efficiency. Much like a domestic microwave oven, SolidWatts’ technology creates electromagnetic waves to heat materials from the inside - a process known as dielectric heating. Unlike traditional heating methods using boilers, ovens and furnaces, SolidWatts’ technology is highly efficient, directly converting 80% or more of the electricity into RF heating power, with minimal energy lost to surrounding infrastructure. And because the system is solid-state, maintenance and service requirements are low and uptime is high. The round saw continued commitment from existing investors Evercurious VC, Kickfund and Axel Carbon Capital, while welcoming a new cohort of backers including Uni.Fund, Investing for Purpose, Loggerhead Ventures, Almanac Ventures and a group of angel investors. “This investment is a tremendous endorsement of our mission to transform how industry consumes energy and allows us to bring RF technology to the power levels and efficiencies that industry actually needs,” said Dr. Markus Aicheler, Founder and CTO of SolidWatts. “Industrial process heating accounts for around 20% of global energy demand and roughly 10% of worldwide CO₂ emissions - yet it remains one of the hardest sectors to decarbonise. As global energy demand rises alongside the AI boom, improving efficiency becomes essential.” Electrifying even 1% of these processes with SolidWatts’ technology could eliminate 36 million tonnes of CO₂ each year - the equivalent of removing 7.8 million cars from the road. SolidWatts was founded in November 2022. Five years ago, when geopolitical tensions exposed the fragility of global energy supply, Dr. Aicheler - a former post-doctoral Fellow at CERN - drew inspiration from CERN’s pioneering use of high-power solid-state RF technology. While CERN leverages this technology for scientific applications, Dr. Aicheler saw the potential for high-power RF to displace fossil fuels in industry. Dr. George Georgiadis, Partner at Evercurious VC, representing the investment group: “SolidWatts powers the future of industrial resilience. Their technology drives efficiency and provides a clear path away from fossil fuel dependency. This investment reflects our mission to back European hard-tech champions that solve real-world problems. We are scaling an innovator born in Europe and built for the world.” (PR) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about SolidWatts SA Company profiles on startup.ch SolidWatts SA Highlights SolidWatts SA Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2024 Winner Venture Kick Related News SolidWatts SA 29.01.2026 09:00 LeadiNNg to Scale-up set to propel promising 5th cohort 20.01.2026 17:15 ZKB Pionierpreis Technopark presents shortlist Related tags

Title: Milestone Hafnova Family app launches
Article_Text: Milestone Hafnova Family app launches 23.03.2026 In response to the sharp rise in cyber scams, Swiss cybersecurity start-up Hafnova is launching a mobile app designed to protect families from online threats. This initiative is a natural extension of Hafnova’s business, which is now shifting towards a B2B2C model thanks to the expertise gained from working with critical infrastructure operators. Phishing, fake text messages and increasingly sophisticated cyberattacks targeting individuals are on the rise, whilst protective tools remain largely inadequate. In Switzerland and elsewhere, individuals have become prime targets, often left without effective protection. In response to this growing threat, Swiss cybersecurity innovator Hafnova is launching Hafnova Family, a mobile app designed to protect families from online threats. Previously reserved for critical infrastructure and highly sensitive environments, Hafnova’s technology is now available to the public, making advanced digital protection accessible to everyone. A free version of the app is now available to download on the App Store and Google Play, allowing users to benefit from essential protection. A premium version offers extended family features and enhanced capabilities, such as blocking access to the dark web or adult content. In line with Hafnova’s mission to make cybersecurity accessible, a portion of the profits generated by the Hafnova Family app will be donated to organizations in Vaud and Geneva that support vulnerable people exposed to online scams. Hafnova will be hosting a public launch event on 26 March 2026 to present its solution (registration link). Shifting towards a hybrid business model Speaking to Startupticker.ch, Edouard Goupy, CEO of Hafnova, explains that the launch of Hafnova Mobile Security for families does not mark a shift towards a purely B2C model. “Our strategy is instead focused on B2B2C, as a natural evolution of our business.” The company is thus responding to specific requests from partners — notably operators of critical infrastructure and telecommunications providers — who wish to offer this type of protection to their own users, particularly on a white-label basis. Furthermore, Hafnova is observing a growing openness towards B2G models, in a context where protecting citizens against cyber threats is becoming a major public concern. The free version of Hafnova Family could also form part of public or institutional support initiatives, to make this protection accessible to as many people as possible. Working with organizations operating critical infrastructure Historically, Hafnova has developed its expertise in critical infrastructure scenarios, particularly in regulated environments and sensitive infrastructure. Edouard Goupy explains: “We work with organizations operating critical infrastructure, as well as with stakeholders in the public sector, public transport and telecommunications. These environments demand high levels of reliability, performance and precision.” He adds: “For confidentiality reasons, we cannot disclose the names of our clients at this stage. Our operations are currently based mainly in Switzerland, with plans to expand across Europe.” Today, Hafnova has a growing team of experts in cybersecurity, artificial intelligence and infrastructure. The company already has several deployments in critical environments, and its technical expertise is particularly recognized in demanding and competitive contexts. Looking ahead, Hafnova’s priorities are to structure and accelerate B2B partnerships, particularly with major accounts and critical infrastructure providers, to strengthen its capabilities in artificial intelligence and real-time detection, expand its presence in Europe, and develop collaborations with public sector organisations to protect citizens. (PR - ES) Picture: cyndidyoder83 / Pixanay 0 Comments LAUNCH More news about Hafnova SA Company profiles on startup.ch Hafnova SA Related News Hafnova SA 15.01.2026 16:00 Diplotools, Impli and Hafnova get FIT funding 30.09.2025 16:50 GENILEM va accélérer GutUp, Hafnova et SeniorContact Related tags

Title: Kandou AI closes $225m Series A round to break memory bottlenecks in AI
Article_Text: Kandou AI closes $225m Series A round to break memory bottlenecks in AI 23.03.2026 Kandou AI, a leader in high-speed, energy-efficient connectivity and system solutions for the AI era, closed its $225mm strategic funding round led by Maverick Silicon with strategic participation from SoftBank Group Corp., Synopsys, Inc., Cadence Design Systems, Inc. and Alchip Technologies Limited. The group of strategic investors shows the strong market conviction in Kandou AI’s mission to solve the critical data movement challenges in AI infrastructure. As artificial intelligence models grow exponentially, the industry faces a fundamental roadblock: GPUs and CPUs need to be connected to an exponentially growing memory footprints leading to memory and interconnect bandwidth becoming key bottlenecks. The performance of AI clusters is increasingly not constrained by compute, but by the speed and efficiency of data interconnects connecting compute infrastructure with memory. Traditional networking and interconnect technologies, designed for a pre-AI world, cannot keep pace. Kandou AI’s silicon proven, revolutionary interconnect technologies are purpose built to solve these key bottlenecks. Lausanne-based Kandou AI secured USD225 million from Maverick Silicon with strategic participation from SoftBank Group Corp., Synopsys, Inc., Cadence Design Systems, Inc. and Alchip Technologies Limited. Several existing investors also participated in the financing round. This new capital infusion will accelerate the company’s strategic priorities: Scaling Production: Manufacturing ramp-up of Kandou AI’s high-performance AI connectivity chips Expanding Customer Engagement: Deepening partnerships with leading hyperscale and AI infrastructure customers globally Advancing the Roadmap: Fueling the next generation of Kandou AI’s chip and IP portfolio to enable multi-terabit connectivity “This funding round is a clear validation of our vision from our partners and customers. It accelerates our goal of transforming AI hardware through products built on our groundbreaking copper-based interconnect and system IP.” said Srujan Linga, Co-Founder and CEO of Kandou AI. “Our initial products will focus on AI systems and high-speed rack connectivity piercing the 448G barrier and beyond, with plans to expand into broader AI infrastructure over time. We are excited to partner with leading hyperscale and enterprise customers to bring these next-generation AI systems to market.” Kandou AI’s unique advantage lies at the intersection of information theory and semiconductor design. Its patented signaling and SerDes technologies fundamentally reimagine how data moves across copper interconnects, delivering breakthrough improvements in bandwidth, reach, and energy efficiency. By unlocking dramatically higher performance from copper, Kandou AI enables build out of scalable AI systems at a fraction of the cost and power helping democratize AI infrastructure and the widespread adoption of AI across the world. “The performance and scalability of next-gen AI clusters will be defined by the ability to move massive amounts of data between chips in an energy efficient way. This is where Kandou AI’s unique strength lies” said Manish Muthal, Senior Managing Director at Maverick Silicon, “we are proud to be supporting their vision of transforming AI system design to enable build out of scalable, energy-efficient AI systems of the future.” The funding announcement follows a period of significant execution for Kandou AI, including, successful tapeout of its next-generation SerDes technology, increased momentum in its existing retimer businesses, expansion of global engineering footprint with a new design center in Hyderabad, India and addition of key industry veterans to its executive team. Title: Milestones and Enhanced Governance drive dEEGtal forward
Article_Text: Milestones and Enhanced Governance drive dEEGtal forward 01.04.2026 Geneva-based dEEGtal, a company specializing in health technology that develops artificial intelligence solutions aimed at improving care in neurology and psychiatry, has reached significant milestones in its development and announced the appointment of entrepreneur in the medical device sector and venture investor Ann Bunnenberg to its board of directors as an independent member. A seasoned medical device entrepreneur, investor, lawyer and business advisor, Ann Bunnenberg’s experience in the neurotechnology sector aligns closely with dEEGtal’s mission to unlock the clinical value of brain signals through artificial intelligence. Her insights into product development, international commercialization, and strategic partnerships will help guide the company as it expands its collaborations with hospitals, research institutions, and technology partners. “I am excited to join the Board of dEEGtal at such an important moment in the company’s evolution,” said Ann Bunnenberg. “The team is developing an innovative approach to interpreting EEG data that has the potential to significantly improve how neurological conditions are detected and managed. I look forward to supporting the company as it brings this technology to clinicians and patients.” dEEGtal’s technology is built on large-scale EEG datasets collected across leading European and American clinical centers and combines brain signal analysis with clinical data to unlock new diagnostic and predictive capabilities across neurology and psychiatry. The company’s first application focuses on improving care pathways following a first seizure, with the goal of enabling faster and more accurate identification of patients at risk of epilepsy. The appointment of Ann Bunnenberg reflects dEEGtal’s commitment to strengthening its strategic leadership and governance as the company continues to scale its technology and expand its clinical and commercial footprint. Significant milestones achieved Contacted by Startupticker.ch, CEO Eric Ménétré explains that dEEGtal has already been able to validate its software on data that were collected from 7 epilepsy centers indeed, in the context of an academic research project that ended before the incorporation of dEEGtal. And the momentum is growing as the CEO tells us: “We also built strong collaborations with leading hospitals in Basel (we are starting a study to evaluate the efficiency of our platform in a real first seizure unit context). We also have an ongoing study which started recently in Vall d’Hebron hospital in Barcelona with a target of about 350 patients. There, we will evaluate our platform in an emergency center’s context, guiding the early triage decisions. We also started a collaboration with a large hospital in Galveston (Texas), UTMB, from where our US clinical trials will be led. In total, as pre-clinical trial evidence, we will have tested our AI model on about 2000 patients.” At dEEGtal, the team is building an artificial intelligence platform designed to enable machines to understand brain signals, starting with electroencephalography (EEG). The company’s first clinical application focuses on epilepsy care, where medical decisions are often delayed despite the widespread use of EEG. By combining large- scale brain signal datasets with advanced AI models, dEEGtal aims to help clinicians identify patients at risk more rapidly and improve clinical decision-making in critical settings such as emergency departments. Ambitious roadmap ahead Next steps for dEEGtal include performing its clinical trials in the US, which will allow the company to become a commercial stage company on this key market. dEEGtal also has a CHF 4-5 million seed round open that should close in Q2 2026. After that, the company led by Eric Ménétré will exploit the full potential of its large brain model to tackle other challenges in other pathologies, populations or industries. Title: PAVE Space raises $40M to disrupt satellite deployment
Article_Text: PAVE Space raises $40M to disrupt satellite deployment 25.03.2026 PAVE Space, a Swiss space infrastructure company, today announced it has raised USD 40 million in seed funding to develop a new generation of spacecraft designed to move satellites rapidly between orbits. The new funding will help PAVE Space move from development into real-world testing and early commercial operations. The investment ranks among the largest seed financings in the global space sector in recent years. Lausanne-based PAVE Space is building a family of orbital transfer vehicles (OTVs) capable of transporting satellites from low Earth orbit (LEO) to higher-energy destinations such as geostationary orbit (GEO), medium Earth orbit (MEO), or lunar trajectories in less than 24 hours — addressing one of the emerging logistical bottlenecks of the rapidly expanding space economy. Today, most rockets deliver satellites only to low Earth orbit. From there, spacecraft must rely on their own electric propulsion systems to slowly climb to their operational orbit, a process that can take six to twelve months and delay revenue-generating services while tying up capital. With more than 12,000 satellites already in orbit and thousands more launched each year, demand for rapid and flexible mobility between orbits is growing quickly. PAVE Space is building the infrastructure to enable it. Solving the logistics bottleneck in orbit PAVE Space's orbital transfer vehicles will move satellites from low Earth orbit to their final operational orbit in less than 24 hours. Its flagship system, a heavy kickstage vehicle, delivers them to higher-energy orbits quickly and reliably. By replacing the traditional electric orbit-raising process, the platform significantly reduces mission timelines while lowering overall mission cost. PAVE's architecture uses storable bipropellants rather than cryogenic fuels, eliminating boil-off constraints inside the fairing, and supporting long-duration missions that cryogenic systems cannot serve. For satellite operators, the impact is substantial. Services can be brought online months earlier, avoiding the inefficiencies and opportunity costs associated with slow orbital transfer. Beyond commercial missions, this capability is also becoming increasingly critical for defense and institutional customers, who require rapid and flexible mobility in orbit. Alongside the heavy kickstage system, PAVE is also developing a smaller mobile platform designed for responsive missions and dual-use applications, enabling satellites and payloads to move rapidly between orbital positions. A new generation of European space engineers PAVE Space was founded in Lausanne in 2024 by Julie Böhning (CEO) and Jérémy Marciacq (CTO), who have been building rockets together since their student years at EPFL. The pair previously co-founded the Gruyère Space Program, the first European reusable rocket initiative. With just CHF 250,000 in sponsorship funding, the team designed and built a reusable rocket demonstrator that completed 53 test flights in 2024. That experience shaped PAVE’s engineering philosophy: maintain full control over the vehicle stack and iterate rapidly through real-world testing. Today the company develops propulsion systems, avionics, control algorithms and structural design internally, enabling rapid engineering cycles and tight integration across the platform. During the past four months, the team has designed, built and tested the propulsion system for its first mobile platform. The company also recently opened a dedicated propulsion test facility — a former Alpine power plant converted into a rocket engine test site — where PAVE will conduct its first rocket engine firings later this year. In parallel, the company is preparing its first in-space demonstration mission scheduled for October. Commercial demand is also building rapidly. The company has already secured 8 reservation agreements with satellite operators and manufacturers and is engaged in discussions with several major industry players. Scaling Europe’s orbital logistics infrastructure The funding round was led by Visionaries Club and Creandum, with participation from Lombard Odier Investment Managers, Atlantic Labs, Sistafund, b2venture, ACE Investment Partners, Ilavaska Vuillermoz Capital, Pareto & Motier Ventures. It ranks among the largest seed financings in the global space sector in recent years. The new funding will allow PAVE Space to: accelerate development of its heavy kickstage logistics platform execute its first in-orbit technical demonstration mission expand its engineering and operations teams prepare its first commercial orbital logistics missions conduct the first ground firing of its propulsion system With a rapidly growing team of engineers and space industry specialists, PAVE is now hiring across propulsion, avionics, mission design and business roles. Building the logistics backbone of the space economy “The space economy is entering an industrial phase where logistics will become as critical in orbit as they are on Earth,” said Julie Böhning, CEO and Co-Founder of PAVE Space. “Our ambition is to build the infrastructure that allows industries to move, operate and scale beyond Earth - while ensuring Europe retains sovereign capabilities in this next strategic domain.” Investors see PAVE as a key infrastructure player emerging at the intersection of several powerful trends: sovereign European space access, accelerating defence investment and the rise of a global space logistics economy. “Getting to space has become routine but moving once you’re there has not.” said Marton Sarkadi Nagy, Partner at Visionaries Club. “PAVE is building the logistics platform that will enable the next generation of commercial and industrial missions in space.” A strategic capability for Europe’s sovereign space ambitions PAVE’s development comes at a time when Europe is placing renewed emphasis on sovereign capabilities in space and beyond. Governments across the continent are investing heavily in space and defense programmes, with more than €55 billion allocated to European defense spending in the coming years and major initiatives underway to strengthen Europe’s autonomy in critical infrastructure sectors. Within this context, orbital mobility is emerging as a strategic capability. Today, Europe lacks an independent logistics provider capable of enabling mobility and responsiveness in and between orbits. PAVE aims to fill that gap. The company’s logistics vehicles are designed to operate with multiple launch providers - including Falcon 9, Ariane 6 and the Japanese MHI - providing European institutions and satellite operators with a flexible and launcher-agnostic mobility layer in space. This approach allows satellite manufacturers, telecom operators and defence organisations to reach strategic orbits faster and more efficiently while reducing dependency on foreign systems. Title: Lancôme and Timeline spark new skincare era
Article_Text: Lancôme and Timeline spark new skincare era 30.03.2026 Together with Swiss longevity biotech Timeline, Lancôme is driving a revolution in skin longevity. At the heart of this collaboration is Mitopure, a breakthrough molecule developed by Timeline. Backed by 15 years of research, it is clinically proven to re-energize mitochondria through cellular renewal. This partnership marks a shift from a reactive approach to aging toward a proactive vision of beauty. The goal is no longer just to correct signs of time, but to restore the skin’s biological vitality from within. Longevity is no longer just about living longer; it's about maintaining vitality, resilience, and strength as we age. In beauty, this evolution means changing how we view the role of skincare — expanding the perspective to encompass the visible biological signs of how skin ages, at every stage of life. Skin is not merely aesthetic. As the body's largest organ, it reflects overall health span. While chronological age is fixed, biological age is dynamic, measurable, and influenceable. By targeting visible biological aging signs, skincare can be seen as a pillar of longevity science, offering the opportunity to act on how skin's appearance evolves as we age. At the forefront of longevity science with Timeline Lancôme is redefining how beauty approaches aging, shifting from "looking younger" to supporting long-term feelings of vitality and confidence. Drawing on 90 years of scientific heritage and the advanced biological expertise of the L'Oréal Groupe, Lancôme pioneers Longevity Integrative Science, a proactive approach that targets the visible manifestations of aging mechanisms to support renewal, resilience, and healthy-looking skin over time. Through strategic open-innovation partnerships, including Swiss biotech company Timeline and Korean beauty tech startup NanoEntek, Lancôme bridges cutting-edge biotech with high-performance consumer skincare, effectively setting our standard for the beauty industry's future. New Absolue Longevity MD validated by dermatologists Formulated with Mitopure by Timeline — the #1 selling longevity Urolithin-A supplement — now introduced into advanced skincare. Absolue Longevity MD is Lancôme's first dermatologist-validated skincare range grounded in Longevity Integrative Science. Designed to reverse the skin's visible biological age at every life stage, it targets visible aging signs including firmness, suppleness, radiance, fine lines, and smoothness helping to preserve the look of strength, vitality, and radiance over time. The skincare products are validated in partnership with Lancôme's advisory board of dermatologists, ensuring thorough review of testing protocols and results; comprehensive ingredient screening for efficacy, safety, and skin compatibility. Mitopure by Timeline: from biotech to skincare A central pillar of longevity is mitochondrial health. Mitochondria generate cellular energy, enabling renewal and resilience. As skin ages, mitochondrial efficiency declines, contributing to visible signs of aging. Mitopure is Timeline's proprietary, highly pure form of Urolithin-A, a postbiotic widely recognized in the longevity field. Backed by more than $50 million in R&D and 15 years of research, Mitopure powers Timeline's supplement, recommended by a collective of longevity doctors & specialist. It enters skincare on a global scale for the first time through Lancôme. Inspired by biotech innovation, this launch delivers high-performance topical formulas, expanding longevity skincare beyond niche applications. Three targeted intervention models Integrated across the ANTICIPATE, INTERCEPT, and RESET intervention models, Mitopure acts as a foundational platform for the whole line. It complements each compound found in the models, helping to support the look of energized, healthy skin throughout its lifespan. Building on this foundation, Lancôme goes beyond a single ingredient, creating proprietary compounds that balance proactive and corrective approaches differently at each stage of the skin's aging trajectory. These compounds are precisely calibrated to respond to the visible biological shifts that occur as skin evolves over time. Title: UBS makes a strategic investment in Artificialy
Article_Text: UBS makes a strategic investment in Artificialy 02.04.2026 Lugano-based Artificialy announced an a strategic investment from UBS, strengthening the partnership between Artificialy and the global banking group. Alongside the investment, UBS plans to significantly expand its collaboration with Artificialy through an enhanced commercial relationship, working to further scale and embed AI solutions across the firm. Artificialy supports organizations at every stage of their AI maturity level, from strategic AI transformation consulting and opportunity mapping, to co-developing advanced AI solutions as long-term partners, to delivering ready-to-use tools like its PrivateAI Suite. The company was founded in 2020 and focusses on AI solutions in production environments. The investment underscores UBS’s commitment to advancing artificial intelligence as a core capability across the firm and builds on an already close partnership between the two organizations. Daniele Magazzeni, Group Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer at UBS, said: “At UBS, we see AI as a key driver of our growth strategy and are committed to deploying it at scale to deliver meaningful benefits for our clients, employees and stakeholders. Our investment in Artificialy builds on a strong partnership and reflects our conviction in the importance of responsible and innovative AI. By working closely with Artificialy, we look forward to exploring new opportunities to apply AI solutions within our organization, supporting Switzerland’s growing AI ecosystem and continuing to deliver innovative, client-focused solutions.” Alberto Delorenzi, Chairman of Artificialy, commented: “This is a significant milestone for Artificialy. Welcoming a shareholder of UBS’s calibre sends a clear signal of confidence in our strategy and growth trajectory. This partnership gives us the backing to pursue our ambitions at a new scale – in Switzerland and beyond.” Luca Gambardella, Co-Founder and CEO of Artificialy, added: “UBS is not only investing in our company – they are also one of our most valued clients. That combination of capital and operational collaboration will help us ground our growth in real world deployment and measurable impact. We look forward to continuing to deliver AI solutions for UBS.” (Press release / SK) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Artificialy SA Company profiles on startup.ch Artificialy SA Related News Artificialy SA 17.10.2025 09:00 New leadership drives growth in biotech and AI startups 05.06.2025 16:30 Abacus investiert in Tessiner KI-Consulting-Startup Related tags

Title: Plair attracts investment from bioMérieux
Article_Text: Plair attracts investment from bioMérieux 27.03.2026 bioMérieux, a global leader in the field of in vitro diagnostics, has invested more than CHF 1 million in Plair, a scale-up company offering a breakthrough solution for real-time microbial detection and air sampling. PLAIR sees this partnership as a major endorsement of its flagship innovation, RAPID-C+, and a decisive step toward setting a new global standard for cleanroom monitoring. Plair’s flagship innovation, RAPID-C+, is a unique real-time viable particle counter that sets a new industry standard in pharmaceutical environmental monitoring. RAPID-C+ is already commercially available. This partnership reinforces bioMérieux’s commitment to driving innovation in environmental monitoring and supporting the evolving needs of the pharmaceutical industry. RAPID-C+ combines real-time counting of viable and total particles with certified continuous sampling on standard agar media, enabling microbial identification and supporting faster, more-informed decision making in cleanroom environments. As part of this collaboration, bioMérieux Senior Vice President of Pharma Quality Control Michael Reynier will join Plair’s Board of Directors. “Our investment in Plair is a strategic step forward in our ambition to be the partner of choice for advancing pharmaceutical quality control,” said Reynier. “RAPID-C+ is a breakthrough innovation that complements our portfolio and supports our customers in delivering critical therapies quickly and safely to patients.” Geneva-based PLAIR will continue working closely with its global ecosystem of partners and distributors to bring RAPID-C+ to pharmaceutical manufacturers worldwide. “An investment from a global leader like bioMérieux brings unique synergies to Plair, creating significant value for all our stakeholders, partners, and RAPID-C+ users,” said Plair CEO Denis Kiselev. “The acceleration of the bio-fluorescent particle counter market demands that quality managers and microbiologists receive clear regulatory support and scientific guidance to adopt this new method. We believe this endorsement by bioMérieux will become a strong signal to the industry, ultimately resulting in better, safer medicines for patients.” This investment reflects bioMérieux’s strategy to diversify and strengthen the Pharma Quality Control offering, which is built on a partner-first approach and over 60 years of scientific excellence. With a global presence in more than 160 countries and a team dedicated to innovation, bioMérieux is recognized as a trusted reference by pharmaceutical leaders worldwide. The family-owned company is headquartered in Marcy-l'Étoile, France, close to Lyon. (Press release / SK) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Plair SA Company profiles on startup.ch Plair SA Highlights Plair SA Winner Venture Kick Related News Plair SA 05.09.2025 14:20 Crowd-Support für weiteres Wachstum 08.07.2025 09:06 Grössere Brötchen backen mit Geld von der Crowd Related tags

Title: Amazon acquires robotics start-up RIVR
Article_Text: Amazon acquires robotics start-up RIVR 20.03.2026 Following an investment two years ago, Amazon has acquired ETH Zurich spin-off RIVR Technologies AG. The company has developed and trained their quadrupedal robots to walk, drive, stand upright on two legs and manipulate packages with wheeled end effectors. The robots are in use in several countries. Rivr is pioneering General Physical AI by deploying robots in last-mile delivery. Currently, delivery drivers can spend most of their time navigating short distances from their vans to customers' front doors, often managing multiple nearby drop-offs. RIVR’s autonomous robotic companion is designed to streamline this process by handling these final steps, reducing the need for repetitive short-distance walking, alleviating driver fatigue while increasing parcel throughput and efficiency. In 2024, Jeff Bezos and the Amazon Industrial Innovation Fund invested in RIVR as part of a USD 22 million funding round. Amazon has now acquired the ETH spin-off. The tech news site The Information was the first to report on the acquisition. The transaction was confirmed to CNBC. An Amazon spokesperson told CNBC in a statement that the acquisition “reflects our commitment to a continued investment in research” and efforts to improve safety for its delivery employees. In a LinkedIn post RIVR CEO Marko Bjelonic commented: "I’m excited to share that RIVR, an Amazon company is now part of Amazon, marking the start of an exciting new chapter for our team. This step will accelerate our vision of building General Physical AI through doorstep delivery, bringing robotics and AI closer to real-world deployment at scale." The deal is likely to further boost interest in Switzerland as a hub for robotics. The Amazon Industrial Innovation Fund has also already identified another promising start-up in Switzerland. It has invested in Algorized. The technology of the Lausanne-based start-up allows robots to "see" through occlusion, dust, and darkness - navigating complex, shared human environments while eliminating unnecessary stops. (Stefan Kyora) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about RIVR Technologies AG Company profiles on startup.ch RIVR Technologies AG Highlights RIVR Technologies AG Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2025 Related News RIVR Technologies AG 01.09.2025 16:48 New Startup Innovation Projects set to advance their technologies 22.08.2025 09:00 RIVR-Roboter liefert Döner in Oerlikon Related tags

Title: GENILEM accélère quatre jeunes pousses romandes prometteuses
Article_Text: GENILEM accélère quatre jeunes pousses romandes prometteuses 01.04.2026 DentalReport, H2REMA, Même Pas Peur! et The Undiet Plan bénéficieront d’un accompagnement global et personnalisé pour une durée allant jusqu’à 3 ans, combinant coaching stratégique, mises en relation ciblées et soutien opérationnel, pour structurer et accélérer leur développement. Sélectionnées à l’issue d’un processus exigeant, elles ont su démontrer à la fois la pertinence de leur solution, leur caractère innovant et des premiers signaux de traction sur leur marché. Le jury a également porté une attention particulière à la qualité des équipes fondatrices, à leur capacité d’exécution et à leur potentiel de développement. Actives dans les domaines de la santé, du digital et de la cleantech, elles rejoignent aujourd’hui une promotion dynamique et diversifiée de 48 entreprises en cours d’accompagnement, ainsi qu’une communauté de plus de 200 alumni DentalReport : Améliorer l’adhésion des patients aux soins dentaires Domaines d’innovation : santé & digital DentalReport répond à un défi central de la pratique dentaire : la difficulté pour les patient·es de comprendre des diagnostics souvent complexes, générant incompréhension, anxiété et refus de soins. Issue de plus de douze ans d’expérience clinique, la solution portée par Catherine Cante et Quentin Capelle propose une plateforme SaaS capable de transformer ces diagnostics techniques en rapports visuels, clairs et pédagogiques, adaptés à chaque patient·e. Cette innovation permet de rétablir une relation de confiance au cœur du soin, tout en améliorant significativement la performance des cabinets : +53 % de taux d’acceptation des devis et un gain de temps quotidien de plus d’une heure par praticien, sans modification de leur infrastructure existante. H2REMA : Simplifier l’accès à l’hydrogène vert Domaine d’innovation : cleantech H2REMA s’attaque à un obstacle clé de la transition énergétique industrielle : le coût élevé et la complexité logistique de l’hydrogène, aujourd’hui majoritairement produit de manière carbonée et transporté sur de longues distances. En développant des électrolyseurs AEM de nouvelle génération, l’entreprise permet aux industriels de produire leur propre hydrogène vert directement sur site, de manière décentralisée et à moindre coût. Cette innovation technologique, combinée à un modèle “hydrogen-on-demand”, élimine jusqu’à 50 % des coûts liés au transport et au stockage, tout en facilitant une adoption progressive grâce à des solutions modulaires. À la clé : une énergie plus propre, plus accessible, et une réduction significative des émissions de CO₂, avec environ 9 kg évités pour chaque kilo d’hydrogène produit. Même Pas Peur ! : Vers une vie plus épanouie pour les enfants malades et leurs familles Domaines d’innovation : santé & social Même Pas Peur ! ambitionne de transformer la manière dont les familles et leur entourage accompagnent les enfants atteints de maladies chroniques, afin de leur permettre de mener une vie plus sereine et épanouissante. Face à un manque de formation des proches et à des systèmes de santé sous tension, la solution propose une approche innovante combinant intelligence artificielle, design et sciences comportementales. Grâce à un moteur d’IA générative vidéo, la plateforme permet de créer en quelques minutes des formations personnalisées, adaptées à chaque enfant et à chaque contexte, afin de rendre tout·e accompagnant·e — parent·e, proche, enseignant·e ou babysitter — rapidement autonome et confiant. En facilitant l’entourage à prendre le relais, Même Pas Peur ! redonne aux familles des espaces de répit, favorise l’inclusion des enfants et contribue à réduire l’isolement et les inégalités liés aux maladies chroniques infantiles. The Undiet Plan : Apprendre à manger pour rompre avec les régimes à répétition Domaine d’innovation : santé The Undiet Plan part d’un constat simple : 95 % des régimes alimentaires échouent à long terme et contribuent à entretenir un cycle de frustration et de reprise de poids. À contre-courant de ces approches restrictives, la solution propose une méthode fondée sur l’éducation métabolique, combinant accompagnement premium, suivi quotidien et personnalisation avancée. L’objectif : permettre à chacune et chacun de comprendre les mécanismes de son corps, de reprendre le contrôle sur son alimentation et de construire des habitudes durables, sans dépendre de régimes à répétition. En se positionnant sur la prévention plutôt que sur le traitement, The Undiet Plan fondé par Caroline Roch apporte une réponse concrète à un enjeu de santé publique majeur, marqué par la progression du surpoids et des maladies métaboliques. Neuf projets prometteurs se dévoilent lors du Pitch de présélection En préambule au Comité de sélection, neuf entreprises candidates au programme d’accélération de GENILEM ont pris part à l’avant-dernière étape du processus. En 3 minutes, elles ont présenté leur solution innovante devant un public de chef·fes d’entreprises et d’entrepreneur·euses expérimenté·es — une opportunité unique de tester la compréhension, l’attrait et le potentiel de leur projet : Lexania : Outil IA pour aider les locataires à faire respecter leurs droits Orchestral : Gestionnaire de paiements pour PME digitales Mikasa : Plateforme d’agrégation d’offres immobilières Pass Senior : Carte avantages pour les séniors en Suisse Phonemia : Application de jeux musicaux interactifs inspirés du beatbox pour aider les enfants à progresser dans leur parole Pommander : Solution olfactive pour expériences immersives Rivelock : Plateforme suisse de paiement sécurisé pour prestations de service Swiway : Mini skate pliable pour la mobilité douce du dernier kilomètre 360skillvue : Plateforme digitale de dépistage précoce des troubles musculo-squelettiques (TMS) et des risques psychosociaux pour les entreprises (PR) 0 Comments SUPPORT More news about H2REMA Sàrl Company profiles on startup.ch H2REMA Sàrl Related News H2REMA Sàrl 19.08.2025 12:00 Ten high-potential cleantech startups selected for Munich investor roadshow 03.02.2025 09:00 Fünf Startups erhalten Förderung vom Pionierfonds Principal Investments 2026 The Swiss Venture Capital Report 2026 offers key insights into the latest trends shaping the investment landscape. Title: Mobilezone übernimmt Apfelkiste
Article_Text: Mobilezone übernimmt Apfelkiste 20.03.2026 Die mobilezone holding ag übernimmt die AK Group, in deren Besitz sich die E-Commerce Plattform Apfelkiste.ch und die auf Entwicklung von Eigenmarken und Beschaffung von Produkten für den Retailmarkt fokussierte Marein AG befinden. Der Transaktionspreis (Enterprise Value) beträgt rund 180 Millionen Franken. 2021 hatte sich die Private Equity Gesellschaft Invision an der AK Group beteiligt. INVISION hat sich im Jahr 2021 an der AK Group beteiligt und das Unternehmen seither als aktiver und unternehmerischer Partner begleitet. Gegründet worden war die Plattform Apfelkiste ursprünglich 2011. In enger Zusammenarbeit mit dem Gründer Pierre Droigk und dem weiteren Management-Team wurden wesentliche strategische Meilensteine erreicht: Die E-Commerce Plattform Apfelkiste.ch wurde konsequent ausgebaut und das Produktsortiment auf über 60’000 sofort verfügbare Produkte erweitert. Durch gezielte Trenderkennung und Sortimentserweiterung gelang es, die Plattform über das Kerngeschäft hinaus erfolgreich zu diversifizieren und auszubauen. 2024 wurde die Marein AG akquiriert. Das Unternehmen hat umfassende Kompetenzen im Trendscouting, in der Entwicklung von Eigenmarken und in der Beschaffung von Produkten aufgebaut. Mit eigenen Brands wie I AM CREATIVE, Esmée oder Paladin Safe ist das Unternehmen heute breit im Sortiment des Schweizer Detailhandels vertreten. Die AK Group beschäftigt heute rund 100 Mitarbeitende und erzielte im Jahr 2025 einen Umsatz von über CHF 100 Mio. bei einem EBITDA von rund CHF 20 Mio. Nun hat INVISION VI ihre Beteiligung an der AK Group AG, einschliesslich der Tochtergesellschaften SkySale Schweiz GmbH (Betreiberin der E-Commerce Plattform Apfelkiste.ch) und Marein AG, an die börsenkotierte mobilezone holding ag («mobilezone») veräussert. Die Transaktion generiert einen attraktiven Return für den INVISION VI Fonds und seine Investoren. Der Transaktionspreis (Enterprise Value) beträgt rund CHF 180 Mio. Der Vollzug der Transaktion wird bis Ende Juni 2026 erwartet. Änderungen in der operativen Führung, bei den Mitarbeitenden oder den Standorten sind nicht geplant. Pierre Droigk, Gründer und bisheriger Group CEO der AK Group, erklärt: «INVISION war für uns ein wertvoller Partner, der uns nicht nur mit Kapital, sondern vor allem mit strategischem Sparring und operativer Unterstützung begleitet hat. Gemeinsam haben wir Apfelkiste zu einer der führenden E-Commerce Plattformen der Schweiz entwickelt. Mit mobilezone startet nun ein spannendes neues Kapitel, in dem wir unsere Stärken bündeln und das Kundenerlebnis weiter ausbauen können.» Christian Hemmrich, Partner bei INVISION, kommentiert: «Wir sind stolz auf die gemeinsam mit Pierre und dem gesamten Team erzielte Entwicklung der AK Group. Aus einem erfolgreichen, gründergeführten E-Commerce Unternehmen ist eine professionell aufgestellte und diversifizierte Gruppe mit über CHF 100 Mio. Umsatz geworden. Die Veräusserung an mobilezone stellt einen hervorragenden strategischen Fit dar und ermöglicht der AK Group, ihr volles Potenzial als Teil einer börsenkotierten Gruppe auszuschöpfen.» Markus Bernhard, Exekutiver Delegierter des Verwaltungsrats, ergänzt: «Seit vielen Jahren verfolgen wir die beeindruckende Erfolgsgeschichte von Apfelkiste. Dass es dem Unternehmen gelungen ist, auch nach dem Corona-Boom das Geschäft weiter stark auszubauen, spricht für die Qualität des Geschäftsmodells und die umsichtige Unternehmensführung. Wir sind überzeugt, dass sich mobilezone, Apfelkiste und MAREIN ausgezeichnet ergänzen und freuen uns, dass wir unsere Stärken für die Zukunft zusammenlegen und so unser Geschäft gemeinsam weiterentwickeln können.» (Press release / SK) Bild (L-R): Pierre Droigk, Gründer AK Group, und Markus Bernhard, Exekutiver Delegierter des Verwaltungsrats mobilezone holding 0 Comments FINANCING More news about SkySale Schweiz (Apfelkiste.ch) Related News SkySale Schweiz (Apfelkiste.ch) 10.06.2025 17:00 Neue Power für Startup-Geschäftsleitungen 19.05.2021 08:38 Invision beteiligt sich an SkySale Schweiz Related tags

Title: Lobby raises USD 2.2 million to automate group bookings across the travel industry
Article_Text: Lobby raises USD 2.2 million to automate group bookings across the travel industry 01.04.2026 Lobby’s AI-driven platform automates the entire process of group bookings to ensure faster response times, more accurate quotes, among others. The proceeds from the funding round will be used to expand beyond the hospitality sector with the Meetings, Incentives, Conferences and Exhibitions (MICE) vertical, experiences and attractions business, cruises and airlines. Group bookings are a growing revenue priority for travel providers, typically generating more per transaction than individual bookings. However, group bookings are complex and slow to process due to manual workflows. Founded in Zürich in 2025, Lobby has built an AI platform that handles the full lifecycle of a group booking request. The company’s solution ensures faster response times and, therefore, higher conversion rates, more accurate quotes and automated follow-ups on unanswered or declined offers. In particular the platform solves one of the most persistent operational challenges: B2B and group bookings that arrive via e-mail, one of the most important sales channels for hotels, MICE industry, means of transportation, as well as experience and activities. Lobby turns incoming e-mail requests into accurate bookings in 30 seconds. Lobby's platform integrates with Gmail, Outlook and existing property management systems. Customers can choose between full automation or a human-in-the-loop model, in which staff review and approve quotes before they are sent. Lobby has raised USD 2.2 million in funding from Founderful. As part of the investment, Pascal Mathis, Co-Founder of the venture capital firm and of GetYourGuide with wide experience in scaling startups, will join Lobby’s Board of Directors. “Lobby is tackling one of the most complex and valuable segments in travel: group bookings” says Pascal Mathis, Co-Founder and Partner of Founderful. “What stood out to us is their ability to move beyond surface-level automation and execute workflows across systems. The travel experience of the founder team helps to build a solution which solves a real problem of the industry.” After successfully closing the investment round, Lobby plans to expand beyond the hospitality sector with the Meetings, Incentives, Conferences and Exhibitions (MICE) vertical, experiences and attractions business, cruises and airlines as its main goals. As part of its expansion, the company is recruiting various new team members. “Having Founderful’s trust gives us even greater strength to take Lobby to the next level: beyond the hospitality sector and into new verticals across the travel industry” explains Romy Abbrederis, CEO and Co-Founder of Lobby. “With our engine, we will help them automate processes that were previously considered too complex and unlock new levels of efficiency and revenue for our customers.” (Press release/AJ) Photo: Lobby founders L-R: Alex Mugrauer, Romy Abbrederis, Muhammad Abdullah 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Lobby AG Company profiles on startup.ch Lobby AG Related News Lobby AG 02.06.2025 17:16 MassChallenge Switzerland presents 2025 cohort Related tags

Title: CHF 1 million to Scale Miros Swiss Network of Connected Workpods
Article_Text: CHF 1 million to Scale Miros Swiss Network of Connected Workpods 30.03.2026 miros, a Lausanne-based startup founded in 2023 out of the EPFL ecosystem, announced the closing of a CHF 1 million Pre-Seed funding round from various business angels supporting the vision. The company will use the funds to deploy a network of connected and bookable workpods across Switzerland, with a target of more than 100 units by the end of 2026. miros is building a network of connected workpods to bring privacy, calm and comfort into public and semi-public spaces. Through its hardware and software platform, including a mobile app to locate, book and access pods, the company addresses the needs of a workforce that is increasingly mobile and flexible. “We start with pods, but the ambition goes far beyond that,” said Dr. Fabio Zuliani, Founder and CEO of miros. “We are looking at how commercial real estate itself can become flexible, modular and on-demand. The pod is just the first building block. Seeing it already used throughout Switzerland and beyond, makes that vision feel very real.” The round follows a manufacturing partnership with Ducommun Menuisiers, initiated through the SyNNergy grant from Innovaud. The pods are designed in collaboration with Lakabi, a Lausanne-based furniture brand, and fully built in Switzerland. This setup brings together engineering, software, design and craftsmanship to scale production while keeping strong attention to detail. In parallel, the project also received support from the Canton of Vaud to develop a new version made entirely from Swiss wood (Bois Suisse), reinforcing its focus on local sourcing and production. Since its inception, miros has deployed 15 workpods and worked with partners including SwissTech Convention Center, Beaulieu, and Hôpital Riviera-Chablais. Through Geneva Airport’s GVA Runway Lab open innovation program, miros was able to test its value proposition in real conditions to refine the oder and confirm strong product-market fit. The company has also taken its first step internationally, with a pod deployed since October at Village by Crédit Agricole in Toulouse, France. These deployments helped validate the product and build the foundations for scalingw with close to 350 hours of usage so far. Neil Chennoufi, Co-Founder and COO of miros, added: “What makes this collaboration special is the connection between people. We know the individuals crafting each pod in the workshop, and we know those installing them on site. It is rare to bridge craftsmanship and startups in such a direct way.” The company plans to announce several new partnerships in the coming months as it continues to expand its footprint across Switzerland. Title: SEALSQ to acquire Miraex
Article_Text: SEALSQ to acquire Miraex 25.03.2026 SEALSQ Corp, a subsidiary of WISeKey International has signed a Letter of Intent (“LOI”) to acquire 100% of the equity interest of Miraex SA, a developer of photonics-based quantum interconnect solutions that bridge quantum processors and quantum networks. The LOI provides for a 60-day exclusivity period during which the parties will conduct confirmatory due diligence and negotiate definitive transaction documents and is binding on the part of Miraex. The Miraex acquisition is expected to be finalized by the end of June 2026, subject to the completion of customary closing conditions and regulatory processes applicable to acquisitions of this nature. The transaction would be carried out using the SEALSQ Quantum Fund as part of SEALSQ’s strategic investment program to build a fully integrated Quantum Vertical Stack. Connecting Quantum Resources Miraex is a developer of photonics-based quantum interconnect solutions, a photonics-based technology layer that enables quantum information to be converted, transmitted, and linked between separated quantum processors and future quantum networks. Based at EPFL Innovation Park, Miraex is one of the very few companies globally working to unlock the full potential of quantum technologies through a Photonic Integrated Circuit (PIC) platform based on Thin Film Lithium Tantalate (TFLT) technology. Miraex operates across three critical quantum verticals: Distributed Quantum Computing (DQS) – enabling the interconnection of distributed quantum processors to achieve scalable quantum computational power. Quantum Sensing – delivering distributed quantum sensing solutions based on photonic entanglement for applications in navigation, geophysics, and defense. Quantum Networking – building globally entangled quantum networks that form the backbone of the future quantum internet. Miraex’s patent portfolio covers direct electro-optical microwave-optical and optical-microwave transduction schemes, as well as traveling wave transducer designs, delivering ultra-high RF-to-optical conversion efficiency. Miraex is also capable of generating photon entanglement across microwave and THz frequency domains separated by a factor of 10,000, a breakthrough capability with profound implications for secure quantum communications and distributed quantum processing. Completing the SEALSQ Quantum Vertical Stack SEALSQ’s vision for its Quantum Vertical Stack encompasses the full spectrum of quantum technology, from quantum-secure semiconductors and post-quantum cryptography (PQC) chips to quantum hardware, and quantum interconnectivity. Miraex’s quantum interconnect platform addresses a pivotal layer of this stack: Quantum Interconnect Layer: Miraex’s technology enables the conversion between microwave (the operating domain of superconducting quantum processors) and optical frequencies (the medium of choice for quantum communication), thereby bridging quantum computing hardware with quantum networks, a foundational requirement for any distributed quantum architecture. Chip-Level Quantum Photonics: Miraex’s compact TFLT PIC designs provide OEM-ready photonic components that can be integrated into SEALSQ’s semiconductor roadmap, enabling next-generation quantum-safe silicon solutions. Distributed Quantum Sensing for Critical Infrastructure: Miraex’s DQS solutions extend SEALSQ’s offering into the domain of ultra-precise quantum sensing for aerospace, defense, financial services, and critical infrastructure protection. With Miraex integrated into SEALSQ, the Company aims to expand and strengthen its vertically integrated quantum technology stack Reinforcing the SEALSQ Quantum Spatial Orbital Cloud The acquisition of Miraex, once completed, should be a decisive accelerant for the SEALSQ Quantum Space Commercial Quantum Spatial Orbital Cloud (QSOC) initiative, SEALSQ’s flagship program to deploy quantum-secure capabilities across Low Earth Orbit (LEO) and beyond. Miraex’s technology is designed to be uniquely aligned with the requirements of a space-based quantum infrastructure. In particular, Space-Grade Photonic Integration is enabled by TFLT PICs, which are compact, power-efficient, and radiation-tolerant by nature, critical attributes for satellite payloads. Their small form factor enables deployment on nanosatellites and CubeSats within the QSOC constellation. Miraex’s photonics-based technologies support the vision of next-generation quantum infrastructure, including global quantum networking and advanced sensing capabilities. These capabilities align with future space-based architectures by enabling the connection of distributed systems and high-precision sensing applications. Together, they represent key building blocks for the development of scalable quantum networks and services across terrestrial and orbital environments. Daniel Brau, CEO of Miraex, commented, “We are excited about the signing of this LOI and the opportunity to join SEALSQ through the Quantum Fund. We are proud of what we have achieved. Our TFLN PIC platform has been designed from the ground up to be the connective tissue of the quantum ecosystem, linking quantum processors, quantum sensors, and quantum networks into a unified infrastructure. We believe SEALSQ’s global reach, semiconductor expertise, and QSOC vision will accelerate our mission to connect quantum resources at planetary scale.” (PR) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Miraex SA Company profiles on startup.ch Miraex SA Highlights Miraex SA Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2020 Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2021 Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2022 Winner Venture Kick Related News Miraex SA 28.03.2025 11:00 Startup Camp Korea welcomes five startups 06.02.2025 13:30 Ten Swiss tech startups selected for Silicon Valley 2025 roadshow Related tags

Title: Pivotal WECHEER AI to power new business perspectives
Article_Text: Pivotal WECHEER AI to power new business perspectives 26.03.2026 In a milestone move, WECHEER is providing brand managers worldwide with 20 million real-world data points. The data reveals exactly which message, promotion or reward prompted a person to visit a physical shop and purchase a product. In the physical world, where 85% of FMCG sales still take place, WECHEER AI unique insights are set to open new business perspectives. Swiss-born technology company is launching wecheer.ai, a free AI tool trained on 20 million proprietary data points collected over 9 years from real consumer promotion campaigns run with leading global CPG brands such as Mars, Nestlé, Pernod Ricard and Coca-Cola across 20+ markets. "CPG is the most offline industry in the world. And every tool out there is built for the retailer, not the brand. We flipped that. For 9 years we've been collecting data that nobody else has. what drives physical-world consumer behavior. Now we're giving it away for free because we believe every brand manager deserves access to it, not just the Fortune 500s.” says WECHEER Co-founder Karim Choueiri. Democratizing business intelligence Speaking of the business model, Karim Choueri tells Startupticker.ch “The biggest barrier we saw for any brand wanting to run a promotion or loyalty program was that they simply didn't know where to start. what mechanic to use, what reward structure works, how to phase a campaign. So, we made the strategy layer free.” Brand managers can now go to wecheer.ai, describe what they need, and you get a complete promotion strategy in minutes based on real performance data. The AI also recommends whether WECHEER SaaS platform is the right fit to execute that strategy. And because it's now fully digitized and self-serve, it's no longer limited to multinationals. A craft brewery, a local coffee roaster, any brand selling in retail can now set up their own program. WECHEER free AI becomes the front door. Its SaaS execution platform is the business. And for brands that want deeper analysis beyond the free tier, there's a paid Pro version as well. Capturing the complete journey The CPG industry generates $9 trillion in annual sales globally, with the vast majority in physical retail. Yet brand managers have been designing promotions based on gut feeling or benchmarks borrowed from e-commerce. WECHEER's dataset is the first of its kind: 20 million data points capturing the complete journey from digital touchpoint to physical purchase. Until today, this intelligence was available only to WECHEER's enterprise clients. The launch of wecheer.ai makes it accessible to any brand, anywhere. A 5-person startup has today the same access as a Fortune 500 trade marketing director. wecheer.ai is available immediately, free of charge with no credit card required. Title: Covalo sichert sich Finanzierung in Millionenhöhe für nächste Wachstumsphase
Article_Text: Covalo sichert sich Finanzierung in Millionenhöhe für nächste Wachstumsphase 02.04.2026 Das Zürcher Startup Covalo hat in einer Seed-Erweiterungsrunde 3,5 Millionen Euro eingesammelt, angeführt von Hi inov und mit Beteiligung bestehender Investoren. Mit dem frischen Kapital will das Unternehmen seine Plattform für Inhaltsstoff- und Produktdaten ausbauen und KI-gestützte Innovationen weltweit vorantreiben. Im Bereich Körperpflege hängt Innovation stark von Inhaltsstoffdaten ab – doch diese sind über Unternehmen, Systeme und Formate fragmentiert. Ein Produkt kann bis zu 40 Rohstoffe mit jeweils rund 20 zugehörigen Dokumenten enthalten. Diese liegen in unterschiedlichen Strukturen vor, stammen von verschiedenen Lieferanten und nutzen unterschiedliche Nomenklaturen, was Teams in F&E, Regulierung und Einkauf viel Zeit für Datenerfassung, -aktualisierung und Compliance kostet. Gleichzeitig erschwert diese Fragmentierung den effektiven Einsatz von KI und modernen digitalen Workflows. Um den Aufwand zu reduzieren und Prozesse zu beschleunigen, hat das 2021 gegründete Startup Covalo eine Plattform entwickelt, die den Austausch von Inhaltsstoffdaten zentral und strukturiert abbildet. Sie ist direkt in die Systeme von Lieferanten sowie in F&E- und PLM-Prozesse der Marken integriert und ersetzt damit E-Mails, PDFs und Excel-Listen, die bislang den Datenaustausch dominieren. Aktualisiert ein Lieferant beispielsweise ein Compliance-Zertifikat, sehen alle Marken, die diesen Rohstoff nutzen, die Änderung. Wird ein neuer Rohstoff qualifiziert, fliessen die Daten direkt in Formulierungs- und Beschaffungsprozesse ein. Timo von Bargen, Mitgründer und CEO von Covalo erläutert: “Die Branche braucht ein gemeinsames Referenzsystem, in dem Inhaltsstoffdaten einmal strukturiert, einmal verwaltet und überall wiederverwendet werden. Unsere grössten Kunden erweitern ihre Nutzung der Plattform Jahr für Jahr um das Zwei- bis Dreifache weil strukturierte Daten automatisch Mehrwert schaffen“. Heute verbindet Covalo über 1’500 Lieferanten mit mehr als 6’000 Marken wie Givaudan, Symrise, PUIG und La Prairie, und arbeitet mit Organisationen wie Reed Exhibitions, TraceOne sowie der B Corp Beauty Coalition zusammen. Im vergangenen Jahr stieg das Engagement auf der Plattform um 84 Prozent mit 1,3 Millionen monatliche Interaktionen, bei einer Net Revenue Retention von über 145 Prozent. Covalo hat sich nun in einer Seed-Erweiterungsrunde 3,5 Millionen Euro unter der Führung von Hi inov sowie mit Beteiligung der bestehenden Investoren HTGF und seed+speed Ventures gesichert. Mit der neuen Finanzierung will Covalo sein Enterprise-Angebot in wichtigen Märkten weiter skalieren. Ausserdem ist geplant, neue branchenspezifische KI-Agenten einzuführen – darunter Conversational Analytics, Datenextraktion und -anreicherung sowie Compliance-Prüfungen – und die Plattform um zentrale Produkt- und Stammdatenfunktionen zu erweitern. Langfristig ist auch eine Expansion in angrenzende Branchen vorgesehen.
